title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgalagadi District Council (markets) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgalagadi District Council (markets) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Kgalagadi District Council (Markets) Bye-laws.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Establishment of market
provisions.content:
3. Establishment of market
(1) The Council may establish markets within the council area as it considers necessary.
(2) A market shall be under the control and supervision of a commercial officer.

provisions.title: Permit to operate stall in market
provisions.content:
4. Permit to operate stall in market
(1) A person shall not operate any stall in a market within the Council area without a permit issued under these Bye-laws.
(2) Any person who operates a stall without a permit in terms of sub-bye-law (1) commits an offence.

provisions.title: Application for permit to operate stall
provisions.content: 5. Application for permit to operate stall

definitions.definition: a commercial officer, public health officer, physical planning officer, bye law officer, police officer or any other officer appointed by the Council to enforce these Bye-laws
a person appointed by the Council to be a commercial officer for the purpose of these Bye-laws
the area within the jurisdiction of the Council
a health inspector employed by the Government or by the Council
an area where commercial dealings are conducted and shall be established by the council under these Bye-laws
any building, stand, shelter, table, place or plot within a market set aside for the sale of merchandise or the provision of a service to the public
a person by whom a stall is hired under these Bye-laws.
